summary: 10 novel product mentions surfaced from 21 scraped stories, filtered against the 90-day history.

items.name: Penpot
items.source_story_title: Thoughts and feelings around Claude Design
items.summary: A design tool commenters thought fits the agentic era because it treats designs as markup instead of opaque canvas state.
items.why_included: It was named directly as a promising alternative in the Claude Design discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter said PenPot might be sitting pretty in this new agentic era because it uses designs as actual markup rather than a pure canvas model.

items.name: MDV
items.source_story_title: Show HN: MDV – a Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides with data
items.summary: A Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides that HN readers compared to other Markdown-powered publishing tools.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced several concrete adjacent tools, signaling real interest in the space.
items.evidence: Commenters immediately compared it with Marp, Djot, Evidence, and a similar CLI/web solution, sdocs.dev.

items.name: sdocs.dev
items.source_story_title: Show HN: MDV – a Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides with data
items.summary: A similar CLI + web Markdown publishing tool that got a shout-out in the MDV discussion.
items.why_included: It was surfaced as a real-world adjacent solution by a commenter building comparable tooling.
items.evidence: One commenter said their own 'similar cli + web based solution' was sdocs.dev and linked it as a reference point.

items.name: Evidence
items.source_story_title: Show HN: MDV – a Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides with data
items.summary: A Markdown + ClickHouse BI/dashboarding tool that commenters highlighted as easy for both models and humans to work with.
items.why_included: It came up as a concrete, battle-tested alternative in the MDV thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said they work on Evidence, a dashboarding/BI solution built around Markdown and ClickHouse, and that models are good at writing it.

items.name: Marp
items.source_story_title: Show HN: MDV – a Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides with data
items.summary: A simple Markdown-to-slides tool with self-hostable HTML/PDF output that commenters already use in practice.
items.why_included: It was the most directly recommended slide-generation alternative in the MDV discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter said they often use Marp for Markdown slides and liked its mostly syntax-light approach plus HTML/PDF export.

items.name: Djot
items.source_story_title: Show HN: MDV – a Markdown superset for docs, dashboards, and slides with data
items.summary: A clean Markdown superset that commenters described as a well-designed extension path for richer document syntax.
items.why_included: It was praised as an extensible alternative alongside the Show HN itself.
items.evidence: One commenter said they had been enjoying djot.net as a superset of Markdown with front matter, schemas, and code-fence metadata.

items.name: Hetzner
items.summary: A lower-cost hosting provider that people described as a good migration target from DO and Linode.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced direct migration experience and savings, making it a useful infra recommendation.
items.evidence: One commenter said they moved two servers from Linode/DO to Hetzner and got similar savings.

items.name: Fuzix OS
items.summary: A micro-Unix aimed at old and resource-constrained systems.
items.why_included: Commenters saw room for it on very small hardware and said it keeps making steady progress.
items.evidence: A commenter said it may pop up on Raspberry Pi Picos and 8-bit micros even though the docs are rough.

items.name: Interval Calculator
items.source_story_title: Show HN: I made a calculator that works over disjoint sets of intervals
items.summary: A calculator for interval arithmetic over disjoint sets that tries to preserve the inclusion property.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the math angle and suggested related interval-visualization tools.
items.evidence: The author explained the inclusion-property approach, and another commenter called it great while linking a related interval arithmetic graphing calculator.

items.name: Kdenlive
items.summary: A FOSS video editor that commenters said hits a sweet spot between basic editors and DaVinci Resolve.
items.why_included: The thread had multiple strong endorsements from people using it in real production workflows.
items.evidence: One commenter called Kdenlive the perfect sweet spot and said it pairs well with OBS and Audacity for a full FOSS media stack.
